About

SQL first, dashboards second, stories always.

I'm a Computer Science MSc student at Paderborn University, with a background from C-DAC and GH Raisoni Institute of Engineering and Business Management. My focus is data analysis — SQL, Python, Power BI, and Excel — building analysis that leads with the business question, not the model.

My portfolio is built around real, verifiable work: five SQL-driven projects spanning fraud risk, SaaS churn, retail inventory, transportation networks, and a 47-million-row taxi dataset — each one grounded in a real bug, a real data-quality gap, or a real engineering constraint, documented alongside exactly how it was solved.

I approach every dataset the same way: understand the business question first, let the data answer it honestly, and communicate the result clearly enough that someone non-technical can act on it.

Projects

Five pipelines. Five real problems solved.

Credit Card Transaction Risk Analytics
View Repo ↗

SQL-driven fraud risk analysis on 285K+ transactions — fraud rate by hour and amount band, a rolling trend via window functions, and a hand-derived Pearson correlation ranking across 28 anonymized features (SQLite has no native CORR()).

284,807Transactions
0.172%Fraud Rate
5SQL Queries
SQLWindow FunctionsPythonXGBoostSHAP
Subscription Revenue & Churn Analytics
View Repo ↗

Cohort retention, MRR, and churn analysis across a 60,000-customer SaaS base — a multi-step CTE reconstructs retention by signup cohort, and churn is compared across plan tiers (Basic churns at ~55% vs Pro at ~25%).

60,000Customers
1.5M+Billing Events
5.5yrHistory
SQLCTEsCohort AnalysisPower BI
Retail Sales & Inventory Analytics
View Repo ↗

Revenue, margin, and inventory-risk analysis across 800 products — a zone-partitioned RANK() surfaces top performers within each category rather than a global ranking dominated by one high-price category, plus a dynamic low-stock alert system.

271K+Sales Transactions
800Products
3.5yrHistory
SQLRANK()ExcelPower BI
Indian Railways Network Analytics
View Repo ↗

Real, open Indian Railways data — flattened from nested GeoJSON, then analyzed for zone speed, state connectivity, and route complexity. Found and quantified a real data-quality gap: ~51% of station records are missing state/zone metadata, disclosed directly rather than hidden.

5,208Trains
8,990Stations
417KSchedule Stops
SQLGeoJSONData QualityReal Data
NYC Yellow Taxi Trip Analytics
View Repo ↗

Chunked ingestion of 47.2M real taxi trips across 7.4GB of source files without ever loading a full file into memory. Hit and fixed a genuine schema-drift bug — a single-character column casing difference (RateCodeID vs RatecodeID) between 2015 and 2016 files that silently corrupted the database mid-load.

47.2MTrip Records
7.4GBSource Data
~7minFull Load Time
SQLChunked ProcessingSchema HandlingPython
Research

Two projects, written up properly.

Paper

SQL-Driven Characterization of the Indian Railways Network: A Data Quality-Aware Analytical Approach

Full IMRaD writeup of the railways project — methodology, results with figures, and a dedicated discussion of the 51% missing-metadata finding and its implications for any zone-level claim.

Download PDF
Paper

Memory-Bounded SQL Analysis of 47.2 Million NYC Taxi Trips: A Case Study in Chunked Ingestion and Cross-Year Schema Drift

A full case study of the RateCodeID/RatecodeID schema-drift bug — root cause, structural fix, and the verification methodology used before reprocessing the complete dataset.

Download PDF
